tags.name: MuMart (museo sommerso)
tags.description: Underwater art museum with contemporary sculptures anchored on sandy seabed at Golfo Aranci, 4-7m depth

tags.name: Cala Mariolu
tags.description: Protected Gulf of Orosei cove with cave-riddled limestone cliffs, visibility frequently exceeding 30m, groupers at cave entrances
Largest marine cave in Mediterranean with ~10 entrances, 4,000m total length, 0-35m depth; red coral walls, air chambers, lobsters, conger eels
Dramatic vertical walls to 30m in Capo Caccia-Isola Piana MPA; caves, cracks, red coral overhangs, barracuda, amberjack, large groupers

WWII German KriegsTransporter KT12, 834 tons, 60m length. Built Livorno, commissioned May 1943. Torpedoed June 10 1943 by HMS Safari (Royal Navy submarine) on maiden voyage with fuel and vehicles for North Africa. Bow separated and settled 300m away; hull sank after 41 minutes burning. Both sections rest at 27-34m off Orosei. Stern gun, propeller, rudder intact. Groupers, moray eels, scorpionfish resident.
Tavolara-Punta Coda Cavallo MPA established 1997, 15,000 hectares. Paleozoic granite formations; dusky groupers, moray eels, barracuda, damselfish; regulated zones with mooring buoys

Three limestone pinnacles 300m east of Punta del Papa; Papa 1 shallowest at 15m, Papa 2 at 24m, from 45-50m base. Clouds of damselfish, large groupers, amberjack, gorgonian fans. Current always present, sometimes strong; Nitrox recommended.

Granite island in Capo Carbonara MPA; Madonna del Naufrago statue at 10-12m (1979), granite monolith La Nave Romana to 40m, red gorgonian colony at Sandbank of Mezzo. Groupers, barracuda, sea bream, lobsters.
Gateway to Bue Marino sea caves on Gulf of Orosei; halocline from fresh spring water meeting salt sea inside cave chambers; avg 9m depth; stalactites from pre-submersion era; boat access only
Capo Carbonara MPA with granite boulders and pinnacles; large dusky groupers (Epinephelus marginatus), barracuda schools, corb, sparidae; visibility often exceeds 30m
Calm natural bay within Capo Caccia-Isola Piana MPA; posidonia oceanica meadows providing habitat for molluscs and crustaceans including lobsters; accessible shore entry
